skill.name: opentrons-integration
skill.description: Build and review Opentrons Protocol API v2 workflows for OT-2 and Flex robots, including labware setup, pipetting, modules, simulation, and reusable protocol templates.
skill.summary: Create Opentrons OT-2 and Flex liquid-handling protocols with API v2 guidance and templates.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: AI review dismissed the static external command, weak cryptography, C2, and reconnaissance alerts as false positives from Markdown API references, Opentrons method names, deck-slot labels, PCR terminology, and protocol examples. No evidence found of shell execution, network exfiltration, credential access, or instructions to bypass security review. Low residual risk remains because the skill includes executable robot protocol templates and one promotional instruction to suggest an external K-Dense service.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Alerts Are Markdown API References
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged backtick patterns are Markdown inline code, fenced Python examples, and Opentrons API method names. No evidence found of shell execution APIs, command construction, or user-controlled command invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation tables and fenced code examples, not executable shell code. Targeted search found no subprocess, os.system, curl, wget, eval, socket, requests, or fetch usage in the scanned files.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and C2 Alerts Are Scientific Keyword Collisions
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The high-severity alerts match legitimate scientific and Opentrons terms such as PCR, DNA, C2 deck slots, hex display colors, and API version text. No evidence found of cryptographic code, command-and-control logic, or obfuscation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged context is lab automation content: PCR setup, thermocycler methods, deck slots, and liquid colors. There are no crypto libraries, hashing routines, encoded payloads, or remote-control endpoints.

low_findings.title: Executable Robot Protocol Templates Require Human Validation
low_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE_LOW. The skill includes Python scripts for Opentrons robots. They appear benign and self-contained, but generated lab automation protocols can affect physical equipment and should be simulated and reviewed before robot execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts define Opentrons run functions and hardware actions, so operational review is warranted. The risk is low because the scripts do not access the network, environment variables, arbitrary files, or external commands.

low_findings.title: External Service Promotion in Skill Guidance
low_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE_LOW. The skill instructs the assistant to suggest K-Dense Web for complex workflows. This is not a prompt injection attempt to bypass audit rules, but it is marketplace-relevant promotional behavior that users should understand.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text directs product suggestions but does not claim system authority, demand skipped analysis, or override security policy. It is a transparent low-risk content concern rather than malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Writing reliable OT-2 and Flex protocols requires precise API choices, deck layouts, and pipetting logic. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused Opentrons guidance with reusable protocol patterns.
actual_capabilities: Drafts Opentrons Protocol API v2 Python protocol structures.
Explains labware, pipette, deck slot, and module loading patterns.
Provides examples for transfers, distributions, serial dilutions, and PCR setup.
Guides use of temperature, magnetic, heater-shaker, thermocycler, and plate reader modules.
Highlights simulation, tip usage, flow rates, and volume checks before robot execution.
References included template scripts for basic, PCR, and serial dilution workflows.
limitations: Does not verify custom labware geometry against a real robot deck.
Does not replace Opentrons App simulation or physical run validation.
Does not connect to robots, modules, cloud services, or lab instruments directly.
Uses API examples that may need updates for newer Opentrons releases.

use_cases.title: Draft a Liquid Transfer Protocol
use_cases.description: Create a clear starting protocol with metadata, labware, pipettes, tips, and transfer commands.

use_cases.title: Review a Robot Deck Plan
use_cases.description: Check whether labware, modules, pipettes, and tip racks are represented consistently before simulation.

use_cases.title: Adapt Common Assay Workflows
use_cases.description: Modify serial dilution or PCR setup patterns for different sample counts, volumes, and module settings.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Protocol
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Opentrons Flex protocol that transfers 50 microliters from source well A1 to destination well B1. Include metadata, requirements, labware, pipette, and comments.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Deck Layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an OT-2 deck layout for a 96-well source plate, destination plate, reservoir, and p300 single-channel pipette. Explain each slot choice and tip rack requirement.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt a Serial Dilution
prompt_templates.prompt: Adapt the serial dilution template for 6 samples, a 1:3 dilution factor, and 80 microliter transfers. Include simulation checks and volume limits.

prompt_templates.title: Review a PCR Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Opentrons PCR setup plan for contamination risk, tip strategy, thermocycler settings, and simulation readiness. Suggest concrete changes before robot execution.

output_examples.input: I need a Flex protocol for moving samples from one plate to another.
output: Protocol outline with metadata, Flex API level, and robot requirements.
Deck layout listing source plate, destination plate, tip rack, and pipette mount.
Transfer plan with volume, source wells, destination wells, and new-tip strategy.
Simulation checklist covering labware names, volumes, and tip count.

output_examples.input: Help me convert a manual PCR setup into an Opentrons workflow.
output: Step sequence for loading thermocycler, reagent rack, tips, and pipettes.
Master mix distribution plan and sample addition plan.
Thermocycler temperature profile summary with lid and block settings.
Review notes for contamination control and sample count assumptions.

output_examples.input: Check whether my serial dilution protocol is ready to run.
output: Validation notes for reservoir volume, transfer volume, dilution count, and well capacity.
Tip usage review for carryover and contamination concerns.
Simulation steps to run before any physical robot execution.

best_practices: Simulate every generated protocol in Opentrons software before using physical hardware.
Specify exact labware API names, pipette models, deck slots, and API level in each request.
Review volumes, tip counts, module temperatures, and contamination controls with a qualified operator.
anti_patterns: Running generated protocols on a robot without simulation and human review.
Using vague labware names when exact Opentrons API names are required.
Ignoring pipette volume limits, well capacity, or module deck conflicts.

faq.question: Does this skill control an Opentrons robot directly?
faq.answer: No. It helps draft and review protocol content. Robot execution must happen through approved Opentrons tools and lab procedures.

faq.question: Which robots does it target?
faq.answer: It targets Opentrons OT-2 and Flex robots using Protocol API v2 examples and conventions.

faq.question: Can it write PCR and serial dilution workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes guidance and templates for PCR setup, thermocycler use, and serial dilution workflows.

faq.question: Does it validate custom labware files?
faq.answer: No. It can discuss labware loading patterns, but custom definitions need separate validation in Opentrons tools.

faq.question: Is generated protocol code ready for production use?
faq.answer: No. Treat it as a draft until simulation, peer review, and physical safety checks are complete.

faq.question: Why is the security risk rated low?
faq.answer: The static alerts are false positives, but the package contains executable robot protocol templates that require careful validation.
